book_claims.claim: Rich in chlorophyll, folic acid, carotenoids and vitamin K, essential to neutralise excess acidity caused by Western diet
book_claims.mechanism: Folic acid supplementation caused a three-fold reduction in recurrence of precancerous colon lesions (adenomas) in a small RCT

book_claims.claim: Contain lutein and zeaxanthin important for protecting eye tissues and reducing cancer/cataract risk
book_claims.mechanism: Carotenoids lutein and zeaxanthin protect retinal tissue from oxidative damage

supplementary_research.finding: Higher dietary nitrate from green leafy vegetables associated with 15.8% lower risk of cardiovascular disease in a meta-analysis. Nitrate converts to nitric oxide, improving endothelial function.
One serving per day of green leafy vegetables slowed cognitive decline equivalent to being 11 years younger. The MIND study identified leafy greens as one of 10 brain-healthy food groups.
Lutein and zeaxanthin supplementation reduced risk of age-related macular degeneration progression (AREDS2 trial). Confirms the book's eye protection claims for these carotenoids.

Vitamin K1 from leafy greens is essential for both blood clotting and calcium metabolism. Deficiency is linked to arterial calcification and osteoporosis. Most adults eating a Western diet are suboptimally supplied.

culinary_pairings.source: Fat aids absorption of fat-soluble vitamins K, A, and carotenoids; book specifically notes this synergy
Acid offsets bitterness; vitamin C enhances non-heme iron absorption from greens
Sauteed greens with garlic is fundamental across cuisines; complementary organosulfur benefits
Calcium from sesame complements greens; gomae (sesame spinach) is a classic
Toasted pine nuts with sauteed greens in Ligurian cuisine; fat aids carotenoid absorption
Cannellini beans and greens provide complementary amino acids and fiber
Fat from yolk enhances carotenoid absorption; classic combination in shakshuka and frittata

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Fat-soluble. Absorption increases 3-5x when eaten with olive oil. Essential for osteocalcin activation and calcium metabolism.
Fat-soluble carotenoids. Accumulate in retinal macula. Cooking increases bioavailability by breaking cell walls.
Naturally occurring folate is ~50% as bioavailable as synthetic folic acid. Light cooking may improve absorption by denaturing folate-binding proteins.
Converted to nitric oxide via oral bacteria. Mouthwash disrupts this conversion. Do not use antiseptic mouthwash before eating greens.

synergies.description: Fat aids absorption of fat-soluble vitamins and carotenoids
